sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच। परं भूयः प्रवक्ष्यामि ज्ञानानां ज्ञानमुत्तमम्। यज्ज्ञात्वा मुनयः सर्वे परां सिद्धिमितो गताः॥
transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca | paraṃ bhūyaḥ pravakṣyāmi jñānānāṃ jñānam uttamam | yaj jñātvā munayaḥ sarve parāṃ siddhim ito gatāḥ ||
literal_meaning: The Blessed Lord said: Again I shall declare to you this supreme wisdom, the best of all knowledge, knowing which all the sages have attained to supreme perfection.
contextual_meaning: Krishna signals a deeper layer of self-understanding: how the mind is governed by three subtle qualities (guṇas). Recognizing these inner ‘programs’ is the master-key that has allowed every great contemplative to move from conflict to complete psychological freedom.

shadow: Doubt about ever transcending the mind

psychological_state: Ready for meta-cognition—Arjuna is willing to look at the very building-blocks of his thoughts and moods.
trigger_scenario: You keep reacting the same way in relationships or work and wonder 'Why do I keep doing this?'—you’re poised to study the automatic patterns behind the reactions.
anchor_text: Notice how your moods shift through three inner programs—observe them and you step beyond their pull.
